description: Build the curated source shelf for a disease (step 1 of the research pipeline). Broadly searches PubMed + NCBI Bookshelf, an LLM classifies which documents belong on the shelf and the role of each (base consensus / update / subtopic / reference compendium), and the writer replaces guideline_source_documents. The synthesis flow (guideline_synthesis) consumes this shelf. Deterministic recall validation lives OUTSIDE the workflow (scripts/validate_shelf_fd.py).

nodes.label: Start
nodes.description: Entry point. Receives disease_slug + disease_name from the run's initial context.

nodes.label: Search PubMed + Bookshelf
nodes.description: Broad multi-query retrieval (consensus/guideline/management reviews + recent reviews + Bookshelf compendia) → candidate list with metadata + abstracts.

nodes.label: Classify the shelf
nodes.description: LLM selects which candidates belong on the shelf and labels each with kind + role. Set model_name to a stronger model here if recall/recognition needs it.
nodes.prompt:
You are a clinical librarian building the SOURCE SHELF for {{ context.initial.disease_name }} — the small set of documents a specialist would actually rely on.

Candidates retrieved from PubMed + NCBI Bookshelf (each has pmid OR bookshelf, title, authors, journal, year, abstract):
{{ context.gsb-search.candidates }}

Select ONLY the documents that belong on the shelf and label each:
- kind: one of base_consensus (the recognised consensus / best-practice guideline), update (a newer paper that changes a recommendation vs the consensus), subtopic (focused on one sub-area, e.g. a region or complication), reference_compendium (a continuously-updated chapter / textbook such as a GeneReviews entry), or other.
- role: a short human label, e.g. "Base consensus", "Children — update", "Craniofacial", "Reference compendium".
- scope: one line on what it covers; covers: a few topic tags.
- updates_note: when kind=update, one line on what it changes vs the consensus.

Rules:
- Copy pmid / bookshelf / title VERBATIM from a candidate — never invent an identifier.
- Prefer ONE base_consensus. Keep newer updates and important subtopics. Include a reference_compendium when present (e.g. a Bookshelf chapter).
- Drop duplicates, primary research that isn't review/consensus, and clearly off-topic hits.
- A tight, high-signal shelf beats a long one.

nodes.label: Write the shelf
nodes.description: Map classified docs onto guideline_source_documents and replace the disease's shelf (terminal node).
